description:
You are working as a mobile-build engineer who has just taken over the company’s
    on-premise CI server.  
    Under /home/user/mobile_ci/logs/ you will find a set of daily build log files
    for the week 2023-08-01 → 2023-08-07.  
    Your goal is to create a concise, machine-readable weekly health report that the
    release managers can ingest automatically.

    What you have to do
    -------------------
    1. Scan every *.log file that is **already** present in
       /home/user/mobile_ci/logs/ (do NOT invent new data or ignore any file).
    2. Extract from each file the following five fields:
       a. build-id              (the string that comes after “Start build:”)
       b. compile result        (SUCCESS / FAILURE)
       c. test result           (SUCCESS / FAILURE)
       d. final build status    (the value after “Build finished with status:”)
       e. total duration in ms  (the integer after “Duration:”)
    3. From all files combined, compute:
       • TOTAL_BUILDS          – count of log files processed  
       • SUCCESSFUL_BUILDS     – how many have final build status SUCCESS  
       • FAILED_BUILDS         – how many have final build status FAILURE  
       • AVERAGE_DURATION_MS   – arithmetic mean of the “Duration:” values
       • FAILED_BUILD_IDS      – comma-separated list of build-ids whose final
                                 status was FAILURE, in ascending order; write
                                 “NONE” if there were no failures
    4. Write those five summary lines, **exactly in the order shown below and in
       ALL-CAPS**, to a new file called
       /home/user/mobile_ci/reports/weekly_summary_2023-08-01_to_2023-08-07.log

       THE FILE MUST HAVE THIS PRECISE 5-LINE FORMAT (no extra spaces or blank
       lines):

       TOTAL_BUILDS:<single space><integer>
       SUCCESSFUL_BUILDS:<single space><integer>
       FAILED_BUILDS:<single space><integer>
       AVERAGE_DURATION_MS:<single space><integer>
       FAILED_BUILD_IDS:<single space><value>

       Example (illustrative only):
       TOTAL_BUILDS: 4
       SUCCESSFUL_BUILDS: 3
       FAILED_BUILDS: 1
       AVERAGE_DURATION_MS: 82625
       FAILED_BUILD_IDS: c1348

    5. For later debugging, also create
       /home/user/mobile_ci/reports/parse_detail.log that lists, **one line per
       source file**, the file name followed by “→” and the extracted build-id,
       final status, and duration separated by commas, e.g.:

       build_2023-08-01_c1345.log → c1345,SUCCESS,85000

       The parse_detail.log file may contain any number of lines but must end
       with a newline character.

    Constraints
    -----------
    • You are limited to typical Unix command-line utilities available on a
      minimal Debian/Ubuntu system (bash, awk, sed, grep, cut, sort, etc.).
    • You should be able to complete the task with roughly 5–10 commands.
    • Do not alter, move or delete the original log files.
    • Make sure /home/user/mobile_ci/reports/ and the two report files are
      created if they do not already exist.

    Completion criteria (checked automatically)
    -------------------------------------------
    1. The file /home/user/mobile_ci/reports/weekly_summary_2023-08-01_to_2023-08-07.log
       exists and is readable by the current user.
    2. The file contains exactly 5 lines and matches the format specified above.
    3. Numerical values in that file correctly reflect the information contained
       in EVERY *.log file under /home/user/mobile_ci/logs/.
    4. The file /home/user/mobile_ci/reports/parse_detail.log exists, is not
       empty, and each of its lines follows the required pattern
       “<source_file> → <build-id>,<status>,<duration>”.
truth:
<!--
        Ground-truth data used by the automatic grader.
        DO NOT reveal any of this in the public <task> section.
    -->

    <!-- Directory layout that is pre-seeded in the container -->
    <dir path="/home/user/mobile_ci">
        <dir path="/home/user/mobile_ci/logs">
            <file name="build_2023-08-01_c1345.log"><![CDATA[
[INFO] Start build: c1345
[INFO] Step compile: SUCCESS in 45000ms
[INFO] Step test: SUCCESS in 30000ms
[INFO] Step package: SUCCESS in 10000ms
[INFO] Build finished with status: SUCCESS
Duration: 85000ms
]]></file>

            <file name="build_2023-08-02_c1346.log"><![CDATA[
[INFO] Start build: c1346
[INFO] Step compile: SUCCESS in 43000ms
[INFO] Step test: SUCCESS in 28000ms
[INFO] Step package: SUCCESS in 9000ms
[INFO] Build finished with status: SUCCESS
Duration: 80000ms
]]></file>

            <file name="build_2023-08-03_c1347.log"><![CDATA[
[INFO] Start build: c1347
[INFO] Step compile: SUCCESS in 46000ms
[INFO] Step test: SUCCESS in 31000ms
[INFO] Step package: SUCCESS in 9500ms
[INFO] Build finished with status: SUCCESS
Duration: 86500ms
]]></file>

            <file name="build_2023-08-04_c1348.log"><![CDATA[
[INFO] Start build: c1348
[INFO] Step compile: SUCCESS in 47000ms
[INFO] Step test: FAILURE in 32000ms
[INFO] Step package: SKIPPED
[INFO] Build finished with status: FAILURE
Duration: 79000ms
]]></file>
        </dir>

        <!-- reports directory is initially empty -->
        <dir path="/home/user/mobile_ci/reports" />
    </dir>

    <!-- Expected contents of the summary file after the agent finishes -->
    <expected_summary><![CDATA[
TOTAL_BUILDS: 4
SUCCESSFUL_BUILDS: 3
FAILED_BUILDS: 1
AVERAGE_DURATION_MS: 82625
FAILED_BUILD_IDS: c1348
]]></expected_summary>

    <!-- Expected parse_detail.log must include exactly these four lines in any order -->
    <expected_parse_detail_lines>
        build_2023-08-01_c1345.log → c1345,SUCCESS,85000
        build_2023-08-02_c1346.log → c1346,SUCCESS,80000
        build_2023-08-03_c1347.log → c1347,SUCCESS,86500
        build_2023-08-04_c1348.log → c1348,FAILURE,79000
    </expected_parse_detail_lines>
